w:r.w:t: Dual Landing Page — Project Development Document
For use with Claude-powered Smart IDE · Local Development

This document is the single authoritative source for building the ZeroEka dual landing page. It is written specifically for execution inside a Claude-powered smart IDE that reads this document, plans tasks autonomously, and executes code. Every section is structured to be machine-readable as well as human-readable.
Local only — no cloud deployment in this phase
Next.js · Three.js · GSAP ScrollTrigger · Tailwind CSS
Three.js (vanilla, no React Three Fiber — for performance control)
AWS (S3 + CloudFront + Amplify) — not in scope now
Claude AI plans and executes tasks autonomously
No Figma — developer makes creative decisions in-code
Before executing any task, the IDE should internalize the following constraints:
No external 3D model files are required. All Three.js scenes are 100% procedural.
No Figma files exist. The developer owns all visual decisions, guided by the token system in Section 3.
Logos are already available as SVG files in /public/assets/logos/.
Hosting is AWS but is NOT in scope. Build and run locally with `next dev` only.
Scrollytelling is core to both pages — GSAP ScrollTrigger with pinned sections is non-negotiable.
Mobile Lottie fallbacks are required for users without GPU capability. These are separate from the Three.js scenes.
All copy is placeholder until the content team delivers final text. Build with clearly labelled placeholder copy.
Performance is critical — Three.js scenes must not block the main thread. Use OffscreenCanvas where possible.
The IDE must scaffold the repository exactly as described below before writing any component code. Every directory path is intentional.
Root layout — fonts, meta, cookie banner
Shared UI primitives (Button, Badge, Card, etc.)
GSAP animation hooks and scroll controllers
GLSL vertex and fragment shader strings
GSAP timeline factories and ScrollTrigger configs
SVG logos — ZeroEka, IIT Madras badge
Next.js config — headers, redirects, font optimization
Tailwind config extending default theme with tokens
Since there is no Figma, the design token CSS file is the single source of truth for all visual decisions. The IDE must create this file first before any component is built.

This is the most technically complex part of the build. Both scenes are fully procedural — zero external 3D files. The IDE must build these as standalone scene modules in /lib/three/ that are then mounted by React components.
Use the following pattern for all Three.js scenes. This separates Three.js logic from React completely:
Scene class lives in /lib/three/LearnersScene.ts — pure TypeScript, no React imports
React component in /components/three/LearnersCanvas.tsx mounts the canvas and calls the scene
useEffect handles scene init, resize observer, and cleanup (dispose all geometries/materials/textures)
Scene receives a scrollProgress float (0–1) each frame from the scroll controller
Procedural knowledge graph — nodes + edges + particle field
200–250 nodes. Generated with seeded random for reproducibility.
Custom ShaderMaterial with violet glow. See shader spec in Section 4.5.
LineSegments using BufferGeometry. Connect nodes within proximity threshold.
Points geometry — 3000 particles. SphereGeometry distribution across r=50 sphere.
PointsMaterial, size 0.08, color #E2E8F0, opacity 0.6, sizeAttenuation: true
Single large node at origin. Brighter shader, scale pulse animation via GSAP.
Slow orbital drift on x/y axes. Responds to mouse (desktop) via lerp.
DeviceOrientationEvent on mobile. Lerp camera target to device tilt.
WebGLRenderer, antialias: true, alpha: false, powerPreference: 'high-performance'
ResizeObserver on container div. Update camera.aspect + renderer.setSize.
Structured org-map — grid-aligned nodes, hierarchical edges
Force-directed but settled — nodes arranged in team clusters. Not chaotic.
80–100 nodes. Smaller, more structured than learner page.
SphereGeometry(r, 12, 12) — higher poly for enterprise quality feel.
Custom ShaderMaterial with gold glow. Color: #F5A623. See Section 4.5.
LineSegments. Hierarchical connections between cluster heads and members.
Default: (0, 8, 16). Angled top-down perspective for org overview feel.
Minimal — gentle sway only. Enterprise = control, not chaos.
Gold vs Violet palette. Grid floor. Structured layout. This is the visual signal of enterprise vs learner.

Both scenes must respond to scroll progress. The GSAP ScrollTrigger controller sends a normalised float (0.0 to 1.0) to the scene each frame. The scene interpolates camera position and node states based on this value.
Zoom in from z=20 to z=12
Constellation expands from origin — nodes fly outward
Learning path trace — glowing line draws between nodes
Feature section — nodes pulse by group
Testimonials — constellation dims, focus on copy
Rush forward — z=18 to z=2 fast
CTA section — stars rush past, launch effect
Org map assembles — nodes drop into grid positions
Use case section — highlight clusters by department
Analytics section — nodes pulse in sync
CTA — all nodes glow gold simultaneously
Two custom shaders are required — one for learner (violet) and one for enterprise (gold). The shader architecture is identical; only the color uniforms differ. Store in /lib/shaders/.
Standard passthrough. Output gl_Position, pass vNormal and vPosition to fragment.
Fresnel-based glow — stronger glow at silhouette edges of sphere.
uniform float — 1.0 base, animated 0.8–1.4 via GSAP for pulse
uniform float — updated each frame from Date.now() for shimmer
nodeGlow.vert.glsl + nodeGlow.frag.glsl — import as raw strings in webpack
Target 60fps on MacBook Pro M1 and equivalent. 30fps minimum on mid-range mobile.
Detect GPU capability on load using a canvas benchmark. If low-end GPU detected, skip postprocessing bloom.
If WebGL not available, hide canvas and show Lottie fallback immediately.
Dispose all Three.js objects on component unmount: geometry.dispose(), material.dispose(), renderer.dispose().
Use requestAnimationFrame cancellation — store animationId and cancel on cleanup.
Throttle mouse/gyroscope events to 60fps using lodash throttle.
Node count can be reduced to 120 on mobile without noticeable visual loss.
Every section below must be built as a standalone React component in /components/sections/learner/. The Three.js canvas sits in a fixed position behind all sections and is scroll-controlled.
Logo, links (Features/Pricing/About), CTA button (Get Started), mobile hamburger, glass blur on scroll
Three.js canvas (full viewport), headline, sub-headline, 2 CTAs, scroll indicator, IIT Madras badge
Institution logos (IISc, IITM, etc.), user count stat, fade-in on scroll enter
3 pain cards (one-liner each), transition statement, scroll-triggered reveal
Pinned scroll section — 3 steps revealed as constellation path traces in Three.js
Chat UI mockup (static demo), 3 capability pills, product screenshot, scroll reveal
6 cards (icon + headline + 2-line body), hover glow effect, staggered reveal on scroll
Mission badges (6, Phase 2 placeholder), streak counter UI, scrolled reveal — Phase 2
3 quotes, avatar placeholder, institution name, auto-advance carousel
2 tiers (Starter / Pro), monthly/annual toggle, feature list, guarantee line
7 Q&A pairs, accessible expand/collapse, keyboard navigable, smooth height animation
Headline, sub, primary CTA — stars rush animation in Three.js triggers on section enter
Logo, nav links, social icons, legal links, copyright
Same base as learner nav — different CTAs: 'Book a Demo' + 'Download Brochure'
Enterprise org-map Three.js canvas, headline (ROI-led), sub-headline, 2 CTAs, trust badges
3 stat cards with industry data (e.g. '94% of L&D teams struggle with engagement'), transition statement
4 tile grid (Personalised Paths / AI Content / Analytics / Integration) — headline + 2-line body each
4 tabs: Onboarding / Upskilling / Compliance / Leadership — content + visual swap on click
Product screenshot (enterprise admin), 6 annotated metric labels, scroll-triggered reveal
12+ partner logos (placeholder boxes for Phase 2), 'Works with your tools' headline
3 trust bullets (encryption, GDPR/DPDP, hosting), SLA statement, security shield icon
3 tiers (Team / Business / Enterprise), per-seat pricing, feature comparison
8 enterprise-specific Q&A pairs (data security, custom training, SSO, contracts)
'Book a Demo' form (name, email, company, team size), Cal.com embed alternative, all nodes glow gold in Three.js
Shared component with learner page footer
GSAP ScrollTrigger is the engine for all scroll-driven behaviour. This section specifies every scroll trigger on both pages.
Register plugin once in layout.tsx: gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger)
All ScrollTrigger instances must be cleaned up in useEffect return: ctx.revert()
Use gsap.context() to scope all animations — prevents memory leaks
ScrollSmoother is NOT used — native scroll only for accessibility and performance
All animations must respect prefers-reduced-motion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) — collapse all animations to instant state
Pin hero, send scroll progress 0→0.15 to scene
Pin section. 3 steps reveal on scroll
Staggered fade + translateY on each card
Tab content swaps on scroll (not click)
Dashboard screenshot slides in, metrics count up
Form slides up, all nodes glow gold
Position: fixed top-0, full width, z-index: 100
Scroll state (>50px): glass blur — backdrop-filter: blur(12px), background: rgba(5,8,20,0.8)
Transition: all 0.3s ease on background and backdrop-filter
Mobile: hamburger menu at <768px, full-screen overlay menu
Both pages share the same nav base component, with page-specific CTA labels passed as props
Main CTA buttons — 'Get Started', 'Book a Demo'
No border, text only with underline
All buttons: 48px min height (touch target), 0.5rem border radius, cursor: pointer, transition: all 0.2s ease, font-family: var(--font-body).
Hover state: border-color transitions to --color-violet-glow. Box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(124,58,237,0.2).
The Lottie player must only render when Three.js is not available. Use this detection logic:
If null → WebGL unavailable → render Lottie component instead of Three.js canvas
Lottie component must be lazy loaded: const LottiePlayer = dynamic(() => import('./LottiePlayer'), { ssr: false })
Lottie JSON files live in /public/assets/lottie/ — two files: constellation.json + orgmap.json
Both Lottie files are placeholders for Phase 1 — use a simple animated SVG fallback if Lottie JSON not yet available
Fonts must be self-hosted. Never load from Google CDN in production (GDPR + performance).
Place all WOFF2 files in /public/fonts/. Declare @font-face in globals.css with font-display: swap. Preload critical fonts (Clash Display) in the <head> via next/head.
◈ symbol — favicon, nav collapse, VR HUD
Single-colour white — VR overlays, dark cards
A modern browser with WebGL support (Chrome or Firefox recommended for Three.js debugging)
VS Code with ESLint + Prettier extensions
App Router, SSR, API routes, Image optimization
React hooks for GSAP context management
Lottie fallback renderer for no-GPU devices
Dark mode system preference — site is dark-only but respects prefers-color-scheme
PostHog public key — safe to expose
Server-side only — never expose to client
Base URL for OG tags and canonical links
Start local dev server at localhost:3000 with hot reload
Production build — checks for TypeScript errors and unused imports
ESLint — fix all warnings before committing
tsc --noEmit — standalone type check without building
This section is written directly for the Claude-powered IDE. Tasks are ordered by dependency. Do not begin a task until all tasks it depends on are complete.
Initialise Next.js 14 project with TypeScript and App Router
Install all packages from Section 10.2 in one command
Configure Tailwind CSS with custom theme extending token values
Create /styles/tokens.css with all CSS custom properties from Section 3
tokens.css — 9 color tokens, 8 type tokens, 6 spacing tokens
Create full directory structure as defined in Section 2.1
Create /app/layout.tsx with font declarations and metadata
Root layout with Clash Display + Syne loaded
Create .env.local from Section 10.3 with placeholder values
Confirm `npm run dev` starts without errors at localhost:3000
Running dev server — blank page OK at this stage
Build Button component — 3 variants (primary/secondary/ghost) from Section 8.2
Build FeatureCard component with hover glow state from Section 8.3
Build Navigation component — both learner and enterprise variants from Section 8.1
Build Footer component — shared across both pages
Build useDeviceCapability hook — detects WebGL availability and GPU tier
Build Lottie fallback wrapper component from Section 8.4
Write GLSL shaders — nodeGlow.vert.glsl + nodeGlow.frag.glsl per Section 4.5
Build LearnersScene class — nodes, edges, particles, camera rig per Section 4.2
Add bloom postprocessing to LearnersScene (EffectComposer + UnrealBloomPass)
Wrap LearnersScene in React component with cleanup and resize handling
Build EnterpriseScene class — org map, gold shader, grid floor per Section 4.3
Verify both scenes render at 60fps locally before proceeding
Confirmed via Chrome DevTools Performance tab
Build learnerScrollController — all ScrollTrigger bindings from Section 7.2
Build enterpriseScrollController — all ScrollTrigger bindings from Section 7.3
Wire learnerScrollController to LearnersScene camera animation keyframes from Section 4.4
Wire enterpriseScrollController to EnterpriseScene camera animation keyframes
Add prefers-reduced-motion check — all animations must resolve to end state instantly
Build all 13 Learner page sections from Section 5 (Hero through Footer)
Build all 12 Enterprise page sections from Section 6 (Hero through Footer)
Assemble /app/platform/page.tsx — compose all learner sections in order
Assemble /app/enterprise/page.tsx — compose all enterprise sections in order
Bind GSAP scroll controllers to assembled pages
Mobile responsive pass — all sections at 390px viewport
Three.js cleanup audit — verify all geometries/materials disposed on unmount
No memory leaks in Chrome Memory tab
WCAG AA contrast check on all text-on-background combinations
Minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio on body text
Keyboard navigation test — Tab through all interactive elements
Visible focus ring on all focusable elements
prefers-reduced-motion test — disable animations via OS setting
WebGL unavailable test — block WebGL in Chrome flags
Mobile layout test — Chrome DevTools device emulation at 390px
All sections render correctly on mobile
TypeScript strict mode — zero type errors
The following are explicitly excluded from this development phase. The IDE must not attempt to build any of these.
Phase 2 — build locally first, deploy after QA sign-off
Phase 2 — requires Claude/OpenAI API integration and rate limiting
Configure account first — add SDK after pages are stable
Phase 2 — not needed until content team starts editing
Phase 2 — requires UX design of slider interface
Motion designer deliverable — use CSS fallback until received
Product team deliverable — use placeholder images until received
Content team deliverable — use dummy text until received
Phase 2 — requires official SVGs from partner brand guides
Phase 2 — requires Resend account and DNS verification
Phase 2 — implement before go-live, not during dev
Legal team deliverable — create empty route placeholders only
All copy below is placeholder only. It must be clearly commented in code with // TODO: Replace with final copy. Never ship placeholder copy to production.
Learn at the speed of your curiosity
ZeroEka maps your knowledge, adapts to your pace, and builds your constellation of expertise — one insight at a time.
Generic courses. Generic pace. Generic results.
You forget 70% of what you learned last week.
Learning apps built for engagement, not mastery.
Let the AI chart your path
Turn your team's learning into measurable ROI
ZeroEka gives L&D teams real-time visibility into skill gaps, learning velocity, and training outcomes across every team.
68% of L&D leaders say their current LMS lacks personalisation
Employees forget 90% of training content within a week without reinforcement
Companies lose $13.5M annually per 1,000 employees to ineffective training
Enterprise-grade security. Built from day one.
99.9% uptime SLA. SOC 2 Type II in progress. GDPR and India DPDP compliant.
Ready to see ZeroEka in action?
60fps on M1 Mac, 30fps on mid-range mobile Chrome
Three.js scene fully disposed on unmount — no retained geometries
Zero errors in strict mode — no `any` types except third-party workarounds
WCAG AA — 4.5:1 contrast, keyboard nav, focus indicators, reduced-motion
All sections legible and usable at 390px. No horizontal scroll.
No janky scrolling — GSAP animations must not block main thread
Lottie (or CSS) fallback renders when WebGL blocked
No commented-out code blocks. No unused imports.
Every placeholder copy block has a // TODO: Replace with final copy comment
◈ ZeroEka Dual Landing Page — Project Document · For Smart IDE Execution · Confidential · v1.0
